name: El Balcón de Bizkaia – circular desde Durango
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Durango are characterized by varied terrain, including mountainous ascents and scenic loops through the Basque Country. The region features a mix of challenging climbs towards sanctuaries and summits, alongside more moderate paths through rural landscapes. Cyclists can expect routes that traverse hills and offer views of the surrounding natural environment. Best touring cycling routes around Durango The most popular touring cycling route is El Balcón de Bizkaia – circular desde Durango, a 22.5 miles (36.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views over Bizkaia. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Urkiola Sanctuary – Below the summit of Urkiolagirre loop from Durango, a difficult 21.6 miles (34.8 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain towards the Urkiola Sanctuary. Local touring cyclists also love the Church of San Andrés loop from Durango, a 17.1 miles (27.5 km) trail leading through the local landscape, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Touring cycling around Durango is defined by mountainous terrain, cultural landmarks, and rural paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Durango are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 19 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Durango's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Durango?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes available around Durango, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Durango?
A: Touring cycling routes around Durango are characterized by varied terrain, including mountainous ascents and scenic loops through the Basque Country. You'll find a mix of challenging climbs towards sanctuaries and summits, alongside more moderate paths through rural landscapes and valleys.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Durango offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069022949/el-balcon-de-bizkaia-circular-desde-durango" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Balcón de Bizkaia – circular desde Durango&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.5-mile (36.2 km) trail with significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31445318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkiola Sanctuary – Below the summit of Urkiolagirre loop from Durango&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial climbing towards the Urkiola Sanctuary.

Q: What are some moderate touring cycling options in Durango?
A: For a more moderate experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40383077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Andrés loop from Durango&lt;/a&gt;. This 17.1-mile (27.5 km) trail leads through local landscapes and is often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes, providing a less strenuous ride while still enjoying the scenery.

Q: What cultural or natural landmarks can I see along the routes?
A: Many routes offer historical and scenic rewards. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/628313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkiola Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, a significant religious building. Other notable points include mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Muniketa (Montecalvo)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Sarasola&lt;/a&gt;, or summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anboto (1,331 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3334263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkiolaguirre Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40377292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guernica Mural in Gernika – Artzubi Bridge loop from Durango&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 40.1-mile (64.6 km) route. This difficult trail offers an extended journey through the region's landscapes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Durango?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 19 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic rural loops, and the cultural landmarks like the Urkiola Sanctuary that can be found along the routes.

Q: Can I find routes that include mountain peaks or high passes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Durango traverse mountainous terrain, offering access to impressive peaks and passes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40375136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saibigain Summit – Urkiola Sanctuary loop from Durango&lt;/a&gt; takes you towards the Saibi Summit Cross and the Urkiola Sanctuary, providing significant elevation gain and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, several routes are known for their panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069022949/el-balcon-de-bizkaia-circular-desde-durango" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Balcón de Bizkaia – circular desde Durango&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is specifically highlighted for its sweeping views over Bizkaia, making it a popular choice for scenic touring.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Durango?
A: The routes in Durango cater to various ability levels. Out of over 40 routes, you'll find a mix, with 26 classified as difficult, 12 as moderate, and 2 as easy. This ensures options for both seasoned cyclists seeking a challenge and those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through settlements or towns?
A: Yes, some routes will take you through or near local settlements. For example, the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3051352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mañaria&lt;/a&gt; is part of the touring cycling network, allowing you to experience local villages and their surroundings while on your ride.
tips: Very good local food, tortilla, coffee, chorizo, etc. Crossroads for many good ride in the area.

The Balcony of Bizkaia, on the slopes of Mount Oiz, offers an impressive view of the Urdaibai basin. Due to its privileged location you can enjoy wonderful views of the Cantabrian Sea, the Sierra de La Demanda and also the Pyrenees.

Small hermitage located on a hill at 791 meters above sea level, near the top of Monte Oiz. It is a place of great devotion and tradition, and offers stunning panoramic views of the surrounding valleys and mountains.

waypoints.name: View from El Balcón de Bizkaia
